evidence_source: Mays et al. 2015 (Solar Physics 290:1775); Riley et al. 2018 (Space Weather 16:1245); Wold et al. 2018 (J. Space Weather Space Climate 8:A17)
promotion_path: Sprint 5 / corona-3fg manifest population OR Sprint 7 final-validate: DOI-resolve all three citations and confirm specific MAE/sigma values; promote verification_status to VERIFIED_FROM_SOURCE.
notes: z_score is supplementary diagnostic per calibration-protocol.md S4.3.3 — not in pass/marginal/fail composite. Sprint 5 may refine the central value to 10h (MAE-anchored) or keep 14h (range-midpoint) based on Run 1 z-score distribution. Run 1 mean z-score 0.538 supports the 14h placeholder.

evidence_source: Conceptually anchored in Riley et al. 2018 (glancing-CME geometry has larger arrival errors); specific 1.5x multiplier is engineer-derived
promotion_path: Sprint 5 / corona-3fg refit: validate the 1.5x factor against Run 1 T3 corpus glancing-blow events (1 event in current corpus: 2017-09-10-CME002) — too few for statistical refit; corpus extension required. Alternative: substitute a different multiplier supported by a DOI-verified Riley/Mays paper finding.
notes: Halo-angle >=45deg threshold codified at corpus-loader.js:168 (T3_GLANCING_HALO_THRESHOLD_DEGREES). The threshold itself is literature-anchored; the 1.5x multiplier is the engineer's heuristic for the magnitude of widening. Inline at uncertainty.js:201: 'if (isGlancingBlow) sigmaHours *= 1.5;'

evidence_source: Aschwanden & Freeland 2012 (ApJ 754:112) — ~8% in-progress flare reclassification rate
promotion_path: Sprint 7 final-validate: DOI-resolve Aschwanden & Freeland 2012 and confirm the ~8% in-progress flare reclassification rate; promote verification_status to VERIFIED_FROM_SOURCE if confirmed; otherwise demote to HYPOTHESIS_OR_HEURISTIC. Sprint 5 may also refit using Run 1 corpus T1 events that were observed in-progress vs at completion (Run 1 corpus has 0 in-progress events; refit deferred until corpus extension).
notes: Sprint 5 evaluation: Run 1 corpus has 5 T1 events all post-event observations; no in-progress events to refit against. Doubt floor 0.4 + class-boundary 0.2 = 0.6 effective doubt for boundary-straddling in-progress flares.

evidence_source: Veronig et al. 2002 (Solar Physics 208:297) — sub-1% completed-event reclassification (citation requires operator-side verification per empirical-evidence.md S4.2)
promotion_path: Sprint 7 final-validate: DOI-resolve the Veronig 2002 citation (engineer flagged title uncertainty per empirical-evidence.md S1.1); promote verification_status to VERIFIED_FROM_SOURCE if confirmed, OR substitute a different verifiable Veronig paper supporting the post-event flare-class stability claim.
notes: Sub-1% reclassification of completed flares supports the ~0.1 floor. The qualitative claim is well-established in flare-statistics literature even if the specific Veronig 2002 citation needs verification.

promotion_path: Sprint 7 / corona-1ml or future cycle: cross-validate against DONKI FLR final-class divergence statistics over the GOES-R era; promote to literature_derived if a primary citation surfaces.
notes: Engineering-estimated as ~half of complete-event doubt; DONKI cross-validation should reduce residual ambiguity but no primary literature directly bounds the reduction.

evidence_source: Matzka et al. 2021 (Space Weather 19:e2020SW002641) — claim: GFZ definitive Kp 1-sigma ~0.33; engineer offline-curated, exact decimal not source-verified
promotion_path: Sprint 5 / corona-3fg manifest population OR Sprint 7 / corona-1ml final-validate: (a) DOI-resolve Matzka et al. 2021 and confirm the specific sigma=0.33 numerical claim verbatim — on confirmation, promote to confidence=high + verification_status=VERIFIED_FROM_SOURCE + provisional=false; OR (b) substitute a different verifiable Kp-uncertainty source that directly reports the GFZ definitive sigma; OR (c) backtest-derive sigma from the Run 1+ T2 corpus (5 events with both kp_swpc + kp_gfz; corpus extension to 15+ events recommended for stable sigma estimate per calibration-protocol.md S3.2 #4).
notes: CR-1 Round 3 fix: matches empirical-evidence.md S4.2 inline tag at L323 + S4.5 section-level default. The qualitative claim that GFZ definitive Kp has ~3x tighter sigma than SWPC preliminary is well-established in geomagnetic-index literature even without DOI confirmation of the 0.33 decimal. Run 1 T2 GFZ-vs-SWPC convergence 0.963 supports the scale separation.

evidence_source: NOAA SWPC operational Kp documentation (services.swpc.noaa.gov product description) — preliminary sigma ~2x definitive due to fewer station inputs
promotion_path: Sprint 7 final-validate: locate a peer-reviewed primary source for SWPC preliminary Kp sigma; OR backtest-derive sigma from Run 1+ T2 corpus events with both kp_swpc and kp_gfz observed (Run 1 has 5 such events: GFZ-SWPC convergence 0.963 -> implied delta-sigma ~0.34 Kp units).
notes: Operational documentation, not peer-reviewed; medium confidence. Same line as corona-evidence-006 in source — both values are literals on uncertainty.js:134.

evidence_source: Wheatland 2001 (Solar Physics 203:87) — active-region flaring rate range 0.5-8/day; X-class triggers at the upper end (citation requires operator-side verification per empirical-evidence.md S5.2)
promotion_path: Sprint 7 final-validate: DOI-resolve the canonical Wheatland 2001 reference (engineer flagged: may be ApJL 536:L109 from 2000 instead of Solar Physics 203:87 from 2001 per empirical-evidence.md S1.1). Sprint 5 refit deferred: Run 1 T4 corpus has 4 X-class trigger events with bucket distribution 1/2/0/0/0 — insufficient for backtest_derived refit per calibration-protocol.md S4.4.2 (requires 3+ events per bucket).
notes: Sprint 5 may refit using Run 1 T4 corpus events; current corpus has 5 events with bucket distribution skewed to low-count outcomes. Post-refit value expected in 5-10 range pending corpus extension to 15-25 events.

evidence_source: Wheatland 2010 (ApJ 710:1601) — Bayesian posterior decay 0.80-0.90 over multi-day windows
promotion_path: Sprint 7 final-validate: DOI-resolve Wheatland 2010 and confirm the 0.80-0.90 decay band; promote verification_status to VERIFIED_FROM_SOURCE if confirmed.
notes: Same line 102 as corona-evidence-008 — X-class params object holds both lambda and decay literals.

evidence_source: Bain et al. 2016 (ApJ 825:1) — M-class productivity ~half X-class
promotion_path: Sprint 7 final-validate: DOI-resolve Bain et al. 2016 and confirm M-class productivity ratio; promote verification_status if confirmed.
notes: M-class lambda 4 = X-class 8 / 2, derived from Bain et al. 2016's M-vs-X productivity finding. Run 1 T4 corpus has 1 M-class trigger event (2022-01-20 M5.5 -> S1 minor); insufficient for refit.

promotion_path: Sprint 5 / corona-3fg refit considered + DEFERRED: validate against M-class trigger T4 corpus events (1 event in Run 1 corpus: 2022-01-20 M5.5 -> S1 minor); promote to literature_derived once corpus extension reaches 15+ M-class triggers per empirical-evidence.md S5.4 + S9. Refit on n=1 sample is not statistically valid.
notes: Wheatland 2001/2010 supports X-class decay band; M-class extrapolation is engineering-estimated. Single settlement-critical engineering-estimated parameter per empirical-evidence.md S9.

promotion_path: Sprint 7 polish: confirm the default branch is unreachable post-M5+ trigger gate (createProtonEventCascade returns null for sub-M5 triggers per proton-cascade.js:191-192). If unreachable, retire the constant in a follow-up cycle.
notes: The default branch is reachable only via degraded-input paths because M5+ trigger gating prevents sub-M5 flares from opening a T4 theatre. Low confidence is acceptable because the value is operationally unused.

promotion_path: Same as wheatland_lambda_default — retire if default branch confirmed unreachable.
notes: Same line 104 as corona-evidence-012 — default params object.

evidence_source: Burlaga et al. 1981 (JGR 86:6673) — inter-spacecraft Bz consistency 1-5 nT; Tsurutani et al. 1988 (JGR 93:8519) — major-storm Bz threshold 10 nT (divergence threshold should be lower)
promotion_path: Sprint 7 final-validate: DOI-resolve Burlaga 1981 and Tsurutani 1988; verify the 1-5 nT inter-spacecraft consistency claim is a direct paper finding rather than engineer-inferred bridge from broader magnetic-cloud structure findings. Sprint 5 refit considered: Run 1 T5 FP=25% suggests threshold may be too sensitive, but the harness reads pre-annotated divergence_signals from corpus, NOT raw DSCOVR/ACE volatility — so changing this threshold does not change Run 2 numerics. Refit deferred until corpus extension AND harness extension to consume runtime threshold.
notes: Sprint 5 NO-OP per empirical-evidence.md S6.4 + Run 1 architectural reality. The 5 nT threshold is set well below the 10 nT magnetospheric-coupling threshold (Tsurutani 1988) and at the upper bound of inter-spacecraft variability (Burlaga 1981). Run 1 T5 FP=25% is corpus_fp_label-anchored, not threshold-anchored.

evidence_source: NOAA SWPC operational L1-coherence guidance (DSCOVR-ACE handover docs)
promotion_path: Sprint 7 final-validate: locate a peer-reviewed primary source for L1 coherence time of genuine solar-wind features; promote verification_status to VERIFIED_FROM_SOURCE if confirmed.
notes: 30-minute streak length matches typical L1 coherence time for genuine solar-wind features.

promotion_path: Sprint 5 / corona-3fg refit DEFERRED: confirm 24h covers typical CME-passage Bz tail; alternative is parameterize per-event from CME duration estimates at corpus-construction time. Defer until corpus extension provides more T5 windows.
notes: Default detection-window width; no literature directly prescribes this. Rationale: covers a typical CME passage from arrival through Bz tail.

evidence_source: NOAA NESDIS GOES-P EXIS/SEM instrument design + uptime documentation
promotion_path: Sprint 7 / corona-1ml or future cycle: locate a peer-reviewed GOES-R Series mission-performance paper (e.g., in Space Weather or JGR Space Physics) that reports primary-satellite uptime statistics; promote to confidence=high + verification_status=VERIFIED_FROM_SOURCE.
notes: All 5 theatres consume GOES-derived signals; settlement-critical. Operational-doc-only earns medium confidence per empirical-evidence.md S7.4.

evidence_source: Pulkkinen et al. 2013 (Space Weather 11:386) — DSCOVR-ACE cross-validation; citation requires operator-side verification per empirical-evidence.md S1.1 + S7.2
promotion_path: Sprint 5 / corona-3fg manifest population OR Sprint 7 final-validate: DOI-resolve the Pulkkinen 2013 citation (or substitute an equivalent peer-reviewed DSCOVR-ACE cross-validation study); promote to confidence=high + verification_status=VERIFIED_FROM_SOURCE.
notes: Sprint 5 split: Sprint 4 evidence-018 sketched a combined entry covering DSCOVR + ACE; Sprint 5 splits per the per-key expansion target in handoff S9. T3 + T5 settlement consume L1 solar-wind data; settlement-critical.

evidence_source: Pulkkinen et al. 2013 (Space Weather 11:386) — DSCOVR-ACE cross-validation; citation requires operator-side verification per empirical-evidence.md S1.1 + S7.2. ACE aging-instrument adjustment per NOAA SWPC operational documentation.
promotion_path: Sprint 7 final-validate: DOI-resolve the Pulkkinen 2013 citation AND locate operational-doc justification for the specific ACE 0.85 vs DSCOVR 0.90 ratio; promote verification_status if confirmed.
notes: Sprint 5 split from Sprint 4 evidence-018 combined entry. The ACE value reflects aging-instrument adjustment via NOAA SWPC operational documentation; this is OPERATIONAL_DOC_ONLY rather than ENGINEER_CURATED_REQUIRES_VERIFICATION because the qualitative claim 'ACE is older + less reliable than DSCOVR' is well-documented in operational guidance.

evidence_source: Standard probability convention; z=1.96 <-> 95% CI on a standard normal (textbook fact, e.g. Casella & Berger, Statistical Inference 2nd ed.)
notes: CI width affects display, not settlement. Textbook claim — no DOI verification needed. Same multiplier appears at uncertainty.js:65 (fluxHigh), :140-141 (Kp CI), :210-211 (CME CI). Source line 64 is the canonical citation site (first appearance for the flare uncertainty model).

evidence_source: NOAA SWPC operational flare-probability product methodology — operational-doc-only
promotion_path: Sprint 5 / corona-3fg manifest population OR Sprint 7 / corona-1ml final-validate: (a) locate a peer-reviewed primary source for the choice of log-flux as the linearization variable in flare-class threshold-crossing probability models — on confirmation, promote to verification_status=VERIFIED_FROM_SOURCE + confidence=high + provisional=false; OR (b) empirically validate the linearization choice against Run 1+ T1 corpus residuals (compare predicted-vs-observed flare-class probabilities under log-flux vs alternative linearizations) — on completion, promote to derivation=backtest_derived + corpus_hash recorded; OR (c) accept indefinite provisional status if no peer-reviewed source surfaces and empirical refit is deemed unnecessary.
notes: CR-2 Round 3 fix: matches empirical-evidence.md S8.3 demotion to OPERATIONAL_DOC_ONLY. The linearization variable choice is encoded in the function comment at uncertainty.js:95 ('Uses normal approximation on log-flux') and consumed at flareThresholdProbability lines 102-114.

evidence_source: NOAA NESDIS GOES-P EXIS/SEM instrument cross-calibration documentation
promotion_path: Sprint 7 final-validate: locate peer-reviewed GOES-R Series mission-performance paper covering secondary-satellite cross-calibration; promote verification_status if confirmed.
notes: GOES secondary (backup); 0.10 reliability gap from primary reflects backup-instrument operational status during eclipse seasons + maintenance windows.

evidence_source: NOAA SWPC operational documentation for DONKI cross-validation; DONKI's NASA-side curation reduces label ambiguity vs raw SWPC feeds
promotion_path: Sprint 7 final-validate: locate peer-reviewed DONKI validation paper (e.g., Mays / Riley / Bain DONKI-CCMC-scoreboard literature); promote verification_status if confirmed. Note: DONKI is NOT a settlement oracle per theatre-authority.md — 'discovery + labels + correlations + evidence enrichment' role only — so settlement_critical=false is correct.
notes: DONKI's curated nature justifies 0.90 reliability — slightly below GOES primary because DONKI publishes with minutes-to-hours latency vs GOES real-time.

evidence_source: Matzka et al. 2021 (Space Weather 19:e2020SW002641) — GFZ definitive Kp gold-standard ~30-day reanalysis stability
promotion_path: Sprint 7 final-validate: DOI-resolve Matzka 2021 specifically for the reliability claim (separate from the sigma=0.33 claim covered by corona-evidence-006); promote verification_status if confirmed.
notes: T2 regression-tier authority per theatre-authority.md. HIGH confidence reflects GFZ's documented gold-standard status; specific 0.95 decimal is engineer-curated.

evidence_source: NOAA SWPC operational Kp documentation — preliminary Kp reliability bounded by ~3x sigma vs GFZ definitive
promotion_path: Sprint 7 final-validate: locate peer-reviewed SWPC vs GFZ Kp comparison paper; promote verification_status if confirmed.
notes: T2 live-tier settlement uses SWPC provisional Kp. The 0.15 reliability gap from GFZ (0.95) reflects the preliminary-vs-definitive sigma divergence anchoring corona-evidence-006/007.

evidence_source: Veronig et al. 2002 (Solar Physics 208:297) — sub-1% reclassification rate for completed X-class events (citation requires operator-side verification per empirical-evidence.md S4.2)
promotion_path: Sprint 7 final-validate: DOI-resolve Veronig 2002 + verify the X-class reclassification rate; promote verification_status if confirmed.
notes: X-class flares have well-defined detector signatures; reliability 0.95 reflects the sub-1% reclassification rate.

evidence_source: Aschwanden & Freeland 2012 (ApJ 754:112) — M-class reclassification rates support ~0.90 reliability ladder position
promotion_path: Sprint 7 final-validate: DOI-resolve Aschwanden & Freeland 2012 specifically for M-class reclassification rate (separate from the in-progress reclassification claim covered by corona-evidence-003).
notes: M-class flares are intermediate; 0.90 reflects clearer detection than C/B/A but slightly below X-class.

evidence_source: Aschwanden & Freeland 2012 (ApJ 754:112) — C-class reclassification rates support intermediate reliability
promotion_path: Sprint 7 final-validate: DOI-resolve Aschwanden & Freeland 2012 for sub-M-class reclassification rates.
notes: C-class flares have intermediate detection reliability; not settlement-critical because T1/T4 theatres require M5+ trigger (sub-M5 events do not open theatres).

evidence_source: Aschwanden & Freeland 2012 (ApJ 754:112) — B-class background-flux ambiguity reduces classification reliability
promotion_path: Sprint 7 final-validate: DOI-resolve Aschwanden & Freeland 2012 for B-class classification ambiguity.
notes: B-class is near GOES background flux; 0.60 reliability reflects substantial classification ambiguity.

evidence_source: Aschwanden & Freeland 2012 (ApJ 754:112) — A-class events at GOES background flux have substantial classification ambiguity
promotion_path: Sprint 7 final-validate: DOI-resolve Aschwanden & Freeland 2012 for A-class background-vs-flare disambiguation literature.
notes: A-class flares are at or below GOES background flux; 0.40 reliability is the lowest in the ladder. Not settlement-critical because A-class events do not trigger T1 or T4 theatres.
